# SOQL/SOSL Quick Reference

## SOQL Syntax
SELECT field1, field2 FROM Object WHERE condition ORDER BY field LIMIT n

## Common Patterns

### Count records
SELECT COUNT() FROM Case WHERE Status = 'Open'

### Filter by date range
SELECT Id FROM Case WHERE CreatedDate >= 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z AND CreatedDate <= 2025-03-31T23:59:59Z

### Group and aggregate
SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) total FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId HAVING COUNT(Id) > 5

### Join via lookup (parent-to-child)
SELECT Id, Account.Name FROM Contact WHERE Account.Industry = 'Technology'

### Use IN clause
SELECT Id, Name FROM User WHERE Id IN ('005xx1', '005xx2', '005xx3')

### SOSL text search
FIND {search term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Case(Id, OwnerId, IssueId__c, Subject)

## SOSL Rules
- Valid search scopes: ALL FIELDS, NAME FIELDS, EMAIL FIELDS, PHONE FIELDS
- DO NOT use: IN DESCRIPTION FIELDS (not valid)
- Syntax: FIND {term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Object(Field1, Field2)
- Use SOSL when you need to search text content across records

## Important Constraints (Salesforce-specific)
- The Description field CANNOT be used in WHERE clauses (not filterable)
- Use SOSL with IN ALL FIELDS to search text content instead of WHERE Description LIKE
- Date format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ (e.g., 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)
- String values use single quotes: WHERE Status = 'Closed'
- NULL check: WHERE Field != null
- LIKE operator works on: Subject, Name, Email (NOT on Description, Body, TextBody)
- Relationship fields use dot notation: Account.Name, Owner.Name, Contact.Email
- For User lookups: query User object separately, then use Id in WHERE clause
- CaseHistory__c tracks ownership changes: Field__c = 'Owner Assignment', NewValue__c = User Id
- CANNOT use column aliases in ORDER BY — use the aggregate expression directly
  WRONG: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) cnt FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY cnt DESC
  CORRECT: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY COUNT(Id) DESC
- SOSL search terms cannot contain hyphens — remove or replace them with spaces

## Key Object Relationships
- Case.OwnerId -> User.Id (current case owner)
- Case.IssueId__c -> Issue__c.Id (issue category)
- Case.OrderItemId__c -> OrderItem.Id -> Product2.Id (product linked to case)
- CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c -> Case.Id (case ownership history)
- CaseHistory__c.NewValue__c -> User.Id (agent assigned)
- Order.AccountId -> Account.Id
- OrderItem.Product2Id -> Product2.Id
- Lead.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Opportunity.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Territory2.Description contains comma-separated state codes
- UserTerritory2Association links User to Territory2

## Task-Specific Hints

### Case Routing: Finding agent expertise
- Find issue expertise: query CaseHistory__c or Case WHERE IssueId__c matches and Status = 'Closed', GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find product expertise: join Case -> OrderItem -> Product2, GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find workload: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Status != 'Closed' GROUP BY OwnerId

### Handle Time: Calculating duration
- Handle time = ClosedDate - CreatedDate (query both fields from Case)
- Filter by date range on CreatedDate or ClosedDate

### Transfer Count: Tracking ownership changes
- Use CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c = 'Owner Assignment' to count transfers
- GROUP BY CaseId__c to get transfer count per case

### Lead Routing: Territory-based assignment
- Territory2.Description contains state codes (e.g., 'CA,IL,AL')
- UserTerritory2Association links agents to territories
- Match lead's state to territory, then find associated agent

# Additional task context
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).
- Q3: July 1 to September 30 (both inclusive).
- Q4: October 1 to December 31 (both inclusive).
## Seasons
- Winter (2024): December 1, 2024 to February 28/29, 2025 (both inclusive), specifying the year to avoid ambiguity.
- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).
- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. (i.e. begins on the date calculated by subtracting six months from the end_date and ends on the end_date) That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

Output (Turn 1):
<step-back>
- Objects involved: Case, Issue__c, OrderItem, Product2, User
- Relationships: Case.IssueId__c → Issue__c.Id, Case.OrderItemId__c → OrderItem.Id, OrderItem.Product2Id → Product2.Id, Case.OwnerId → User.Id
- Constraints: Description fields are not filterable. Must use Name or SOSL for text search.
- Logical sequence: 1) Find Issue__c matching "Engine Malfunction", 2) Find Product2 matching "Volvo XC60", 3) Query Cases with that IssueId and linked to that product, 4) Group by OwnerId to find top agent
</step-back>
<thought> Starting by finding the Issue__c record for engine malfunction. </thought>
<execute> SELECT Id, Name FROM Issue__c WHERE Name LIKE '%Malfunction%' </execute>

Output (Turn 2):
<thought> Found Issue 'Vehicle Malfunction' with Id 'a03xx1'. Now finding the product. </thought>
<execute> SELECT Id FROM Product2 WHERE Name LIKE '%Volvo XC60%' </execute>

Output (Turn 3):
<thought> Found Product Id. Now finding the agent with most closed cases for this issue. </thought>
<execute> SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE IssueId__c = 'a03xx1' AND Status = 'Closed' GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY COUNT(Id) DESC LIMIT 1 </execute>

# Salesforce instance description
The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2, ProductCategoryProduct, Pricebook2, PricebookEntry, Order, OrderItem, EmailMessage, LiveChatTranscript, Issue__c, CaseHistory__c, Opportunity, OpportunityLineItem, Quote, QuoteLineItem, Contract, VoiceCallTranscript__c, Task, Event, Territory2, UserTerritory2Association, Lead

## The fields available for the objects along with their descriptions and dependencies are:
User
  - Alias: Alias, usually a shortened version of the agent's name
  - Email: Email address of the agent
  - EmailEncodingKey: Email encoding, usually 'UTF-8'
  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'
  - Username: Unique username for the agent in Salesforce
Account
  - Description: Detailed description of the account
  - FirstName: First name of the account
  - Id: ID of the account
  - LastName: Last name of the account
  - Name: Name of the account
  - PersonEmail: Email address of the person associated with the account
  - Phone: Phone number of the account
  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - ClosedDate: Date when the case was closed in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this case. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the case was created in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the case
  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case
  - OrderItemId__c: ID of the Order Item associated with this case
  - OwnerId: The latest owner of the case. References User object
  - Priority: Priority of the case
  - Status: Status of the case, e.g., 'New', 'Working', 'Escalated', etc
  - Subject: Subject of the case
Knowledge__kav
  - FAQ_Answer__c: Content of the knowledge article
  - Id: ID of the knowledge article
  - Summary: Summary of the article
  - Title: Title of the knowledge article
  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to
  - Id: ID of the product category
  - Name: Name of the product category
Product2
  - Description: Description of the product
  - External_ID__c: External ID for mapping purposes
  - Id: ID of the product
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the product is active
  - Name: Name of the product
ProductCategoryProduct
  - Id: ID of the product category product
  - ProductCategoryId: References ProductCategory object
  - ProductId: References Product2 object
Pricebook2
  - Description: Description of the price book
  - Id: ID of the price book
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the price book is active
  - Name: Name of the price book
  - ValidFrom: Start date for the price book in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ValidTo: End date for the price book in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
PricebookEntry
  - Id: ID of the price book entry
  - Pricebook2Id: ID of the price book this entry belongs to. References Pricebook2 object
  - Product2Id: ID of the product associated with this price book entry. References Product2 object
  - UnitPrice: Unit price of the product
Order
  - AccountId: ID of the customer account. References Account object
  - EffectiveDate: Effective date of the order in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format
  - Id: ID of the order
  - OwnerId: ID of the agent who owns the order. References User object
  - Pricebook2Id: ID of the price book this order belongs to. References Pricebook2 object
  - Status: Status of the order, typically 'Draft' or 'Activated'
OrderItem
  - Id: ID of the order item
  - OrderId: ID of the order this item belongs to. References Order object
  - PricebookEntryId: ID of the price book entry for this order item. References PricebookEntry object
  - Product2Id: ID of the product associated with this order item. References Product2 object
  - Quantity: Quantity of the product ordered
  - UnitPrice: Unit price of the product
EmailMessage
  - FromAddress: Sender email address
  - Id: ID of the email message
  - MessageDate: Timestamp when the message was sent in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ParentId: ID of the case related to this email. References Case object
  - RelatedToId: ID that links to objects like Opportunity and Case. Identical to ParentId when associated with a case. References Case and Opportunity objects.
  - Subject: Subject of the email
  - TextBody: Content of the email
  - ToIds: List of recipient IDs
LiveChatTranscript
  - AccountId: ID of the customer account. References Account object
  - Body: Content of the chat transcript
  - CaseId: ID of the case related to this chat. References Case object
  - ContactId: References Contact object
  - EndTime: Timestamp when the chat ended
  - Id: ID of the live chat transcript
  - LiveChatVisitorId: ID of the chat visitor
  - OwnerId: ID of the agent who owned the chat. References User object
Issue__c
  - Description__c: Detailed description of the issue
  - Id: ID of the issue

CaseHistory__c
  - CaseId__c: ID of the case this history entry is related to. References Case object
  - CreatedDate: The date when this change was made, in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Field__c: Specifies the field or type of change. One of ['Case Creation', 'Owner Assignment', 'Case Closed']
  - Id: ID of the case history
  - NewValue__c: The new value after the change. References User object if the type of change is 'Owner Assignment' else null
  - OldValue__c: The previous value before the change. References User object if the type of change is 'Owner Assignment' else null
Opportunity

OpportunityLineItem

QuoteLineItem

# SOQL/SOSL Quick Reference

## SOQL Syntax
SELECT field1, field2 FROM Object WHERE condition ORDER BY field LIMIT n

## Common Patterns

### Count records
SELECT COUNT() FROM Case WHERE Status = 'Open'

### Filter by date range
SELECT Id FROM Case WHERE CreatedDate >= 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z AND CreatedDate <= 2025-03-31T23:59:59Z

### Group and aggregate
SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) total FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId HAVING COUNT(Id) > 5

### Join via lookup (parent-to-child)
SELECT Id, Account.Name FROM Contact WHERE Account.Industry = 'Technology'

### Use IN clause
SELECT Id, Name FROM User WHERE Id IN ('005xx1', '005xx2', '005xx3')

### SOSL text search
FIND {search term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Case(Id, OwnerId, IssueId__c, Subject)

## SOSL Rules
- Valid search scopes: ALL FIELDS, NAME FIELDS, EMAIL FIELDS, PHONE FIELDS
- DO NOT use: IN DESCRIPTION FIELDS (not valid)
- Syntax: FIND {term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Object(Field1, Field2)
- Use SOSL when you need to search text content across records

## Important Constraints (Salesforce-specific)
- The Description field CANNOT be used in WHERE clauses (not filterable)
- Use SOSL with IN ALL FIELDS to search text content instead of WHERE Description LIKE
- Date format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ (e.g., 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)
- String values use single quotes: WHERE Status = 'Closed'
- NULL check: WHERE Field != null
- LIKE operator works on: Subject, Name, Email (NOT on Description, Body, TextBody)
- Relationship fields use dot notation: Account.Name, Owner.Name, Contact.Email
- For User lookups: query User object separately, then use Id in WHERE clause
- CaseHistory__c tracks ownership changes: Field__c = 'Owner Assignment', NewValue__c = User Id
- CANNOT use column aliases in ORDER BY — use the aggregate expression directly
  WRONG: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) cnt FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY cnt DESC
  CORRECT: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY COUNT(Id) DESC
- SOSL search terms cannot contain hyphens — remove or replace them with spaces

## Key Object Relationships
- Case.OwnerId -> User.Id (current case owner)
- Case.IssueId__c -> Issue__c.Id (issue category)
- Case.OrderItemId__c -> OrderItem.Id -> Product2.Id (product linked to case)
- CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c -> Case.Id (case ownership history)
- CaseHistory__c.NewValue__c -> User.Id (agent assigned)
- Order.AccountId -> Account.Id
- OrderItem.Product2Id -> Product2.Id
- Lead.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Opportunity.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Territory2.Description contains comma-separated state codes
- UserTerritory2Association links User to Territory2

## Task-Specific Hints

### Case Routing: Finding agent expertise
- Find issue expertise: query CaseHistory__c or Case WHERE IssueId__c matches and Status = 'Closed', GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find product expertise: join Case -> OrderItem -> Product2, GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find workload: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Status != 'Closed' GROUP BY OwnerId

### Handle Time: Calculating duration
- Handle time = ClosedDate - CreatedDate (query both fields from Case)
- Filter by date range on CreatedDate or ClosedDate

### Transfer Count: Tracking ownership changes
- Use CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c = 'Owner Assignment' to count transfers
- GROUP BY CaseId__c to get transfer count per case

### Lead Routing: Territory-based assignment
- Territory2.Description contains state codes (e.g., 'CA,IL,AL')
- UserTerritory2Association links agents to territories
- Match lead's state to territory, then find associated agent

# Additional task context
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

# Domain Details
## Quarters of the Year
- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).
- Q3: July 1 to September 30 (both inclusive).
- Q4: October 1 to December 31 (both inclusive).
## Seasons
- Winter (2024): December 1, 2024 to February 28/29, 2025 (both inclusive), specifying the year to avoid ambiguity.
- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).
- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. (i.e. begins on the date calculated by subtracting six months from the end_date and ends on the end_date) That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.
Identify the agent with the fewest transfers among those who handled over 3 cases in the last two quarters. Return only the Id of the agent.

<step-back>
- Objects involved: Case, CaseHistory__c, User
- Relationships: Case.OwnerId → User.Id, CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c → Case.Id, CaseHistory__c.NewValue__c → User.Id (for owner assignment)
- Constraints: Description fields are not filterable. Must use Name or SOSL for text search.
- Logical sequence: 1) Find cases handled in the last two quarters, 2) Group by OwnerId to find agents who handled over 3 cases, 3) Count transfers per agent, 4) Identify the agent with the fewest transfers
</step-back>

The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2, ProductCategoryProduct, Pricebook2, PricebookEntry, Order, OrderItem, EmailMessage, LiveChatTranscript, Issue__c, CaseHistory__c, Opportunity, OpportunityLineItem, Quote, QuoteLineItem, Contract, VoiceCallTranscript__c, Task, Event, Territory2, UserTerritory2Association, Lead

User

  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'
  - Username: Unique username for the agent in Salesforce
Account
  - Description: Detailed description of the account
  - FirstName: First name of the account
  - Id: ID of the account
  - LastName: Last name of the account
  - Name: Name of the account
  - PersonEmail: Email address of the person associated with the account
  - Phone: Phone number of the account
  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object

  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this case. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the case was created in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the case
  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case
  - OrderItemId__c: ID of the Order Item associated with this case
  - OwnerId: The latest owner of the case. References User object
  - Priority: Priority of the case
  - Status: Status of the case, e.g., 'New', 'Working', 'Escalated', etc
  - Subject: Subject of the case
Knowledge__kav
  - FAQ_Answer__c: Content of the knowledge article
  - Id: ID of the knowledge article
  - Summary: Summary of the article
  - Title: Title of the knowledge article
  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to
  - Id: ID of the product category
  - Name: Name of the product category
Product2
  - Description: Description of the product
  - External_ID__c: External ID for mapping purposes

ProductCategoryProduct

Pricebook2

PricebookEntry

Order

OrderItem

# SOQL/SOSL Quick Reference

## SOQL Syntax
SELECT field1, field2 FROM Object WHERE condition ORDER BY field LIMIT n

## Common Patterns

### Count records
SELECT COUNT() FROM Case WHERE Status = 'Open'

### Filter by date range
SELECT Id FROM Case WHERE CreatedDate >= 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z AND CreatedDate <= 2025-03-31T23:59:59Z

### Group and aggregate
SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) total FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId HAVING COUNT(Id) > 5

### Join via lookup (parent-to-child)
SELECT Id, Account.Name FROM Contact WHERE Account.Industry = 'Technology'

### Use IN clause
SELECT Id, Name FROM User WHERE Id IN ('005xx1', '005xx2', '005xx3')

### SOSL text search
FIND {search term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Case(Id, OwnerId, IssueId__c, Subject)

## SOSL Rules
- Valid search scopes: ALL FIELDS, NAME FIELDS, EMAIL FIELDS, PHONE FIELDS
- DO NOT use: IN DESCRIPTION FIELDS (not valid)
- Syntax: FIND {term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Object(Field1, Field2)
- Use SOSL when you need to search text content across records

## Important Constraints (Salesforce-specific)
- The Description field CANNOT be used in WHERE clauses (not filterable)
- Use SOSL with IN ALL FIELDS to search text content instead of WHERE Description LIKE
- Date format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ (e.g., 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)
- String values use single quotes: WHERE Status = 'Closed'
- NULL check: WHERE Field != null
- LIKE operator works on: Subject, Name, Email (NOT on Description, Body, TextBody)
- Relationship fields use dot notation: Account.Name, Owner.Name, Contact.Email
- For User lookups: query User object separately, then use Id in WHERE clause
- CaseHistory__c tracks ownership changes: Field__c = 'Owner Assignment', NewValue__c = User Id
- CANNOT use column aliases in ORDER BY — use the aggregate expression directly
  WRONG: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) cnt FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY cnt DESC
  CORRECT: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY COUNT(Id) DESC
- SOSL search terms cannot contain hyphens — remove or replace them with spaces

## Key Object Relationships
- Case.OwnerId -> User.Id (current case owner)
- Case.IssueId__c -> Issue__c.Id (issue category)
- Case.OrderItemId__c -> OrderItem.Id -> Product2.Id (product linked to case)
- CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c -> Case.Id (case ownership history)
- CaseHistory__c.NewValue__c -> User.Id (agent assigned)
- Order.AccountId -> Account.Id
- OrderItem.Product2Id -> Product2.Id
- Lead.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Opportunity.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Territory2.Description contains comma-separated state codes
- UserTerritory2Association links User to Territory2

## Task-Specific Hints

### Case Routing: Finding agent expertise
- Find issue expertise: query CaseHistory__c or Case WHERE IssueId__c matches and Status = 'Closed', GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find product expertise: join Case -> OrderItem -> Product2, GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find workload: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Status != 'Closed' GROUP BY OwnerId

### Handle Time: Calculating duration
- Handle time = ClosedDate - CreatedDate (query both fields from Case)
- Filter by date range on CreatedDate or ClosedDate

### Transfer Count: Tracking ownership changes
- Use CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c = 'Owner Assignment' to count transfers
- GROUP BY CaseId__c to get transfer count per case

### Lead Routing: Territory-based assignment
- Territory2.Description contains state codes (e.g., 'CA,IL,AL')
- UserTerritory2Association links agents to territories
- Match lead's state to territory, then find associated agent

# Additional task context
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).
- Q3: July 1 to September 30 (both inclusive).
- Q4: October 1 to December 31 (both inclusive).
## Seasons
- Winter (2024): December 1, 2024 to February 28/29, 2025 (both inclusive), specifying the year to avoid ambiguity.
- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).
- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. (i.e. begins on the date calculated by subtracting six months from the end_date and ends on the end_date) That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

You are an expert in Salesforce and you have access to a Salesforce instance. You are interacting with the system and an internal user (i.e., an employee of the same company).

# Instructions
- You will be provided a question, the system description, and relevant task context.
- You MUST follow the Step-Back approach:
  1. FIRST, step back and reason abstractly: What Salesforce objects, fields, and relationships are relevant? What constraints exist? What is the logical approach?
  2. THEN, execute SOQL/SOSL queries based on your abstract understanding.
- Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) and Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL) can be used to query the system.
- Your generation should always contain a structured output and NOTHING ELSE. Generate only one action per turn.
- DO NOT generate ANY system observation, you will receive this based on your Action command.
- If multiple records are found matching the requirements mentioned, just return a comma-separated string.
- If no record is found matching the requirements mentioned, just return 'None'.

<step-back>

- What relationships connect them? [describe the joins/lookups needed]
- What constraints apply? [fields that can't be filtered, syntax rules]
- What is the logical sequence? [what to query first, second, etc.]
</step-back>

# Guidelines
- ALWAYS start with <step-back> on your first turn to reason about the problem abstractly.
- In your step-back, identify which objects and fields you need BEFORE writing any query.
- Consider constraints: Description fields cannot be filtered, use SOSL for text search.
- After the first turn, proceed with <thought> and <execute> like normal.
- When you are confident about the answer, submit it with <respond>.
- Always end with a <respond> action containing ONLY the answer, NO full sentences or any explanation.

# Example
Question: Which agent closed the most cases related to "Engine Malfunction" for Volvo XC60?
Output (Turn 1):
<step-back>
- Objects involved: Case, Issue__c, OrderItem, Product2, User
- Relationships: Case.IssueId__c → Issue__c.Id, Case.OrderItemId__c → OrderItem.Id, OrderItem.Product2Id → Product2.Id, Case.OwnerId → User.Id
- Constraints: Description fields are not filterable. Must use Name or SOSL for text search.
- Logical sequence: 1) Find Issue__c matching "Engine Malfunction", 2) Find Product2 matching "Volvo XC60", 3) Query Cases with that IssueId and linked to that product, 4) Group by OwnerId to find top agent
</step-back>
<thought> Starting by finding the Issue__c record for engine malfunction. </thought>
<execute> SELECT Id, Name FROM Issue__c WHERE Name LIKE '%Malfunction%' </execute>

Output (Turn 2):
<thought> Found Issue 'Vehicle Malfunction' with Id 'a03xx1'. Now finding the product. </thought>
<execute> SELECT Id FROM Product2 WHERE Name LIKE '%Volvo XC60%' </execute>

Output (Turn 3):
<thought> Found Product Id. Now finding the agent with most closed cases for this issue. </thought>
<execute> SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE IssueId__c = 'a03xx1' AND Status = 'Closed' GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY COUNT(Id) DESC LIMIT 1 </execute>

# Salesforce instance description
The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2, ProductCategoryProduct, Pricebook2, PricebookEntry, Order, OrderItem, EmailMessage, LiveChatTranscript, Issue__c, CaseHistory__c, Opportunity, OpportunityLineItem, Quote, QuoteLineItem, Contract, VoiceCallTranscript__c, Task, Event, Territory2, UserTerritory2Association, Lead

## The fields available for the objects along with their descriptions and dependencies are:
User
  - Alias: Alias, usually a shortened version of the agent's name
  - Email: Email address of the agent
  - EmailEncodingKey: Email encoding, usually 'UTF-8'
  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'
  - Username: Unique username for the agent in Salesforce
Account
  - Description: Detailed description of the account
  - FirstName: First name of the account
  - Id: ID of the account
  - LastName: Last name of the account
  - Name: Name of the account
  - PersonEmail: Email address of the person associated with the account
  - Phone: Phone number of the account
  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - ClosedDate: Date when the case was closed in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this case. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the case was created in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the case
  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case
  - OrderItemId__c: ID of the Order Item associated with this case
  - OwnerId: The latest owner of the case. References User object
  - Priority: Priority of the case
  - Status: Status of the case, e.g., 'New', 'Working', 'Escalated', etc
  - Subject: Subject of the case
Knowledge__kav
  - FAQ_Answer__c: Content of the knowledge article
  - Id: ID of the knowledge article
  - Summary: Summary of the article
  - Title: Title of the knowledge article
  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to
  - Id: ID of the product category
  - Name: Name of the product category
Product2
  - Description: Description of the product
  - External_ID__c: External ID for mapping purposes
  - Id: ID of the product
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the product is active
  - Name: Name of the product
ProductCategoryProduct
  - Id: ID of the product category product
  - ProductCategoryId: References ProductCategory object
  - ProductId: References Product2 object
Pricebook2
  - Description: Description of the price book
  - Id: ID of the price book
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the price book is active
  - Name: Name of the price book
  - ValidFrom: Start date for the price book in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ValidTo: End date for the price book in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
PricebookEntry
  - Id: ID of the price book entry
  - Pricebook2Id: ID of the price book this entry belongs to. References Pricebook2 object
  - Product2Id: ID of the product associated with this price book entry. References Product2 object
  - UnitPrice: Unit price of the product
Order
  - AccountId: ID of the customer account. References Account object
  - EffectiveDate: Effective date of the order in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format
  - Id: ID of the order
  - OwnerId: ID of the agent who owns the order. References User object
  - Pricebook2Id: ID of the price book this order belongs to. References Pricebook2 object
  - Status: Status of the order, typically 'Draft' or 'Activated'
OrderItem
  - Id: ID of the order item
  - OrderId: ID of the order this item belongs to. References Order object
  - PricebookEntryId: ID of the price book entry for this order item. References PricebookEntry object
  - Product2Id: ID of the product associated with this order item. References Product2 object
  - Quantity: Quantity of the product ordered
  - UnitPrice: Unit price of the product
EmailMessage
  - FromAddress: Sender email address
  - Id: ID of the email message
  - MessageDate: Timestamp when the message was sent in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ParentId: ID of the case related to this email. References Case object
  - RelatedToId: ID that links to objects like Opportunity and Case. Identical to ParentId when associated with a case. References Case and Opportunity objects.
  - Subject: Subject of the email
  - TextBody: Content of the email
  - ToIds: List of recipient IDs
LiveChatTranscript
  - AccountId: ID of the customer account. References Account object
  - Body: Content of the chat transcript
  - CaseId: ID of the case related to this chat. References Case object
  - ContactId: References Contact object
  - EndTime: Timestamp when the chat ended
  - Id: ID of the live chat transcript
  - LiveChatVisitorId: ID of the chat visitor
  - OwnerId: ID of the agent who owned the chat. References User object
Issue__c
  - Description__c: Detailed description of the issue
  - Id: ID of the issue
  - Name: The name/title of the issue
CaseHistory__c
  - CaseId__c: ID of the case this history entry is related to. References Case object
  - CreatedDate: The date when this change was made, in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Field__c: Specifies the field or type of change. One of ['Case Creation', 'Owner Assignment', 'Case Closed']
  - Id: ID of the case history
  - NewValue__c: The new value after the change. References User object if the type of change is 'Owner Assignment' else null
  - OldValue__c: The previous value before the change. References User object if the type of change is 'Owner Assignment' else null
Opportunity
  - AccountId: ID of the customer account. References Account object
  - Amount: Monetary value of the opportunity in the default currency
  - CloseDate: Expected close date of the opportunity
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this opportunity. References Contact object
  - ContractID__c: ID of the contract associated with this opportunity. References Contract object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the opportunity was created, in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the opportunity
  - Id: ID of the opportunity
  - Name: Name of the opportunity
  - OwnerId: ID of the agent who owns the opportunity. References User object
  - Probability: Probability of winning the opportunity
  - StageName: Stage of the opportunity. One of ['Qualification', 'Discovery', 'Quote', 'Negotiation', 'Closed']
OpportunityLineItem
  - Id: ID of the opportunity line item
  - OpportunityId: ID of the opportunity associated with this line item. References Opportunity object
  - PricebookEntryId: ID of the price book entry for this line item. References PricebookEntry object
  - Product2Id: ID of the product associated with this line item. References Product2 object
  - Quantity: Quantity of the product ordered
  - TotalPrice: Total price of the product. Calculated as Quantity * UnitPrice * (1 - Discount/100)
Quote
  - AccountId: ID of the customer account. References Account object
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this quote. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the quote was created, in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the quote
  - ExpirationDate: Expiration date of the quote, in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Id: ID of the quote
  - Name: Name of the quote
  - OpportunityId: ID of the opportunity associated with this quote. References Opportunity object
  - Status: Status of the quote. One of ['Draft', 'Needs Review', 'In Review', 'Approved', 'Rejected', 'Presented', 'Accepted', 'Denied']
QuoteLineItem
  - Discount: Discount applied to the product
  - Id: ID of the quote line item
  - OpportunityLineItemId: ID of the opportunity line item associated with this quote line item. References OpportunityLineItem object
  - PricebookEntryId: ID of the price book entry for this line item. References PricebookEntry object
  - Product2Id: ID of the product associated with this line item. References Product2 object
  - Quantity: Quantity of the product ordered
  - QuoteId: ID of the quote associated with this line item. References Quote object
  - TotalPrice: Total price of the product. Calculated as Quantity * UnitPrice * (1 - Discount/100)
  - UnitPrice: Unit price of the product
Contract
  - AccountId: ID of the customer account. References Account object
  - ContractTerm: Duration of the contract in months
  - CustomerSignedDate: Date when the customer signed the contract, in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the contract terms and conditions
  - Id: ID of the contract
  - StartDate: Date when the contract becomes effective, in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format
  - Status: Status of the contract. One of ['In approval Process', 'Activated', 'Draft', 'Rejected', 'Negotiating', 'Awaiting Signature', 'Signature Declined', 'Signed', 'Canceled', 'Contract Terminated']
VoiceCallTranscript__c
  - Body__c: Content of the voice call transcript
  - CreatedDate: Date when the voice call transcript was created, in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - EndTime__c: Timestamp when the voice call transcript ended, in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Id: ID of the voice call transcript
  - LeadId__c: ID of the lead associated with this voice call transcript. References Lead object
  - OpportunityId__c: ID of the opportunity associated with this voice call transcript. References Opportunity object
Task
  - ActivityDate: Date when the task is due, in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the task
  - Id: ID of the task
  - OwnerId: ID of the agent who owns the task. References User object
  - Priority: Priority of the task. One of ['Low', 'Medium', 'High', 'Urgent']
  - Status: Status of the task. One of ['Not Started', 'In Progress', 'Completed', 'Deferred', 'Waiting']
  - Subject: Subject of the task
  - WhatId: ID of the opportunity associated with this task. References Opportunity object
Event
  - Description: Detailed description of the event
  - DurationInMinutes: Duration of the event in minutes
  - Id: ID of the event
  - IsAllDayEvent: Boolean to mark if the event is an all-day event
  - Location: Location of the event
  - OwnerId: ID of the agent who owns the event. References User object
  - StartDateTime: Start datetime of the event, in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Subject: Subject of the event
  - WhatId: ID of the opportunity associated with this event. References Opportunity object
Territory2
  - Description: A comma separated list of territories/states (e.g. CA,IL,AL)
  - Id: ID of the territory
  - Name: Name of the territory
UserTerritory2Association
  - Id: ID of the user territory association
  - Territory2Id: ID of the territory associated with the agent. References Territory2 object
  - UserId: ID of the agent who is associated with the territory. References User object
Lead
  - ConvertedAccountId: ID of the Account record created when the Lead is converted. References Account object
  - ConvertedContactId: ID of the Contact record created when the Lead is converted. References Contact object

  - Id: ID of the lead

  - Phone: Phone number of the lead
  - Status: Status of the lead. One of ['New', 'Contacted', 'Qualified', 'Proposal', 'Negotiation', 'Closed']


# SOQL/SOSL Quick Reference

## SOQL Syntax
SELECT field1, field2 FROM Object WHERE condition ORDER BY field LIMIT n

## Common Patterns

### Count records
SELECT COUNT() FROM Case WHERE Status = 'Open'

### Filter by date range
SELECT Id FROM Case WHERE CreatedDate >= 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z AND CreatedDate <= 2025-03-31T23:59:59Z

### Group and aggregate
SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) total FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId HAVING COUNT(Id) > 5

### Join via lookup (parent-to-child)
SELECT Id, Account.Name FROM Contact WHERE Account.Industry = 'Technology'

### Use IN clause
SELECT Id, Name FROM User WHERE Id IN ('005xx1', '005xx2', '005xx3')

### SOSL text search
FIND {search term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Case(Id, OwnerId, IssueId__c, Subject)

## SOSL Rules
- Valid search scopes: ALL FIELDS, NAME FIELDS, EMAIL FIELDS, PHONE FIELDS
- DO NOT use: IN DESCRIPTION FIELDS (not valid)
- Syntax: FIND {term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Object(Field1, Field2)
- Use SOSL when you need to search text content across records

## Important Constraints (Salesforce-specific)
- The Description field CANNOT be used in WHERE clauses (not filterable)
- Use SOSL with IN ALL FIELDS to search text content instead of WHERE Description LIKE
- Date format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ (e.g., 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)
- String values use single quotes: WHERE Status = 'Closed'
- NULL check: WHERE Field != null
- LIKE operator works on: Subject, Name, Email (NOT on Description, Body, TextBody)
- Relationship fields use dot notation: Account.Name, Owner.Name, Contact.Email
- For User lookups: query User object separately, then use Id in WHERE clause
- CaseHistory__c tracks ownership changes: Field__c = 'Owner Assignment', NewValue__c = User Id
- CANNOT use column aliases in ORDER BY — use the aggregate expression directly
  WRONG: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) cnt FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY cnt DESC
  CORRECT: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY COUNT(Id) DESC
- SOSL search terms cannot contain hyphens — remove or replace them with spaces

## Key Object Relationships
- Case.OwnerId -> User.Id (current case owner)
- Case.IssueId__c -> Issue__c.Id (issue category)
- Case.OrderItemId__c -> OrderItem.Id -> Product2.Id (product linked to case)
- CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c -> Case.Id (case ownership history)
- CaseHistory__c.NewValue__c -> User.Id (agent assigned)
- Order.AccountId -> Account.Id
- OrderItem.Product2Id -> Product2.Id
- Lead.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Opportunity.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Territory2.Description contains comma-separated state codes
- UserTerritory2Association links User to Territory2

## Task-Specific Hints

### Case Routing: Finding agent expertise
- Find issue expertise: query CaseHistory__c or Case WHERE IssueId__c matches and Status = 'Closed', GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find product expertise: join Case -> OrderItem -> Product2, GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find workload: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Status != 'Closed' GROUP BY OwnerId

### Handle Time: Calculating duration
- Handle time = ClosedDate - CreatedDate (query both fields from Case)
- Filter by date range on CreatedDate or ClosedDate

### Transfer Count: Tracking ownership changes
- Use CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c = 'Owner Assignment' to count transfers
- GROUP BY CaseId__c to get transfer count per case

### Lead Routing: Territory-based assignment
- Territory2.Description contains state codes (e.g., 'CA,IL,AL')
- UserTerritory2Association links agents to territories
- Match lead's state to territory, then find associated agent

# Additional task context
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Quarters of the Year
- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).
- Q3: July 1 to September 30 (both inclusive).
- Q4: October 1 to December 31 (both inclusive).
## Seasons
- Winter (2024): December 1, 2024 to February 28/29, 2025 (both inclusive), specifying the year to avoid ambiguity.
- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).
- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. (i.e. begins on the date calculated by subtracting six months from the end_date and ends on the end_date) That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.

- Logical sequence: 1) Find all cases in the past four quarters, 2) Count transfers for each case, 3) Filter agents managing more than one case, 4) Find the agent with the fewest transfer counts

# Salesforce instance description
The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2, ProductCategoryProduct, Pricebook2, PricebookEntry, Order, OrderItem, EmailMessage, LiveChatTranscript, Issue__c, CaseHistory__c, Opportunity, OpportunityLineItem, Quote, QuoteLineItem, Contract, VoiceCallTranscript__c, Task, Event, Territory2, UserTerritory2Association, Lead

## The fields available for the objects along with their descriptions and dependencies are:
User
  - Alias: Alias, usually a shortened version of the agent's name
  - Email: Email address of the agent
  - EmailEncodingKey: Email encoding, usually 'UTF-8'
  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'
  - Username: Unique username for the agent in Salesforce
Account
  - Description: Detailed description of the account
  - FirstName: First name of the account
  - Id: ID of the account
  - LastName: Last name of the account
  - Name: Name of the account
  - PersonEmail: Email address of the person associated with the account
  - Phone: Phone number of the account
  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - ClosedDate: Date when the case was closed in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this case. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the case was created in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the case
  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case
  - OrderItemId__c: ID of the Order Item associated with this case
  - OwnerId: The latest owner of the case. References User object
  - Priority: Priority of the case
  - Status: Status of the case, e.g., 'New', 'Working', 'Escalated', etc
  - Subject: Subject of the case
Knowledge__kav
  - FAQ_Answer__c: Content of the knowledge article
  - Id: ID of the knowledge article
  - Summary: Summary of the article
  - Title: Title of the knowledge article
  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to
  - Id: ID of the product category
  - Name: Name of the product category
Product2
  - Description: Description of the product
  - External_ID__c: External ID for mapping purposes
  - Id: ID of the product
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the product is active
  - Name: Name of the product
ProductCategoryProduct
  - Id: ID of the product category product
  - ProductCategoryId: References ProductCategory object
  - ProductId: References Product2 object
Pricebook2
  - Description: Description of the price book
  - Id: ID of the price book
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the price book is active
  - Name: Name of the price book
  - ValidFrom: Start date for the price book in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ValidTo: End date for the price book in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
PricebookEntry
  - Id: ID of the price book entry
  - Pricebook2Id: ID of the price book this entry belongs to. References Pricebook2 object
  - Product2Id: ID of the product associated with this price book entry. References Product2 object
  - UnitPrice: Unit price of the product
Order
  - AccountId: ID of the customer account. References Account object
  - EffectiveDate: Effective date of the order in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format
  - Id: ID of the order
  - OwnerId: ID of the agent who owns the order. References User object
  - Pricebook2Id: ID of the price book this order belongs to. References Pricebook2 object
  - Status: Status of the order, typically 'Draft' or 'Activated'
OrderItem
  - Id: ID of the order item
  - OrderId: ID of the order this item belongs to. References Order object
  - PricebookEntryId: ID of the price book entry for this order item. References PricebookEntry object
  - Product2Id: ID of the product associated with this order item. References Product2 object
  - Quantity: Quantity of the product ordered
  - UnitPrice: Unit price of the product
EmailMessage
  - FromAddress: Sender email address
  - Id: ID of the email message
  - MessageDate: Timestamp when the message was sent in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ParentId: ID of the case related to this email. References Case object
  - RelatedToId: ID that links to objects like Opportunity and Case. Identical to ParentId when associated with a case. References Case and Opportunity objects.
  - Subject: Subject of the email
  - TextBody: Content of the email
  - ToIds: List of recipient IDs
LiveChatTranscript
  - AccountId: ID of the customer account. References Account object
  - Body: Content of the chat transcript
  - CaseId: ID of the case related to this chat. References Case object
  - ContactId: References Contact object
  - EndTime: Timestamp when the chat ended
  - Id: ID of the live chat transcript
  - LiveChatVisitorId: ID of the chat visitor
  - OwnerId: ID of the agent who owned the chat. References User object
Issue__c
  - Description__c: Detailed description of the issue
  - Id: ID of the issue
  - Name: The name/title of the issue
CaseHistory__c
  - CaseId__c: ID of the case this history entry is related to. References Case object
  - CreatedDate: The date when this change was made, in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Field__c: Specifies the field or type of change. One of ['Case Creation', 'Owner Assignment', 'Case Closed']
  - Id: ID of the case history
  - NewValue__c: The new value after the change. References User object if the type of change is 'Owner Assignment' else null
  - OldValue__c: The previous value before the change. References User object if the type of change is 'Owner Assignment' else null
Opportunity
  - AccountId: ID of the customer account. References Account object
  - Amount: Monetary value of the opportunity in the default currency
  - CloseDate: Expected close date of the opportunity
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this opportunity. References Contact object
  - ContractID__c: ID of the contract associated with this opportunity. References Contract object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the opportunity was created, in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the opportunity

# SOQL/SOSL Quick Reference

## SOQL Syntax
SELECT field1, field2 FROM Object WHERE condition ORDER BY field LIMIT n

## Common Patterns

### Count records
SELECT COUNT() FROM Case WHERE Status = 'Open'

### Filter by date range
SELECT Id FROM Case WHERE CreatedDate >= 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z AND CreatedDate <= 2025-03-31T23:59:59Z

### Group and aggregate
SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) total FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId HAVING COUNT(Id) > 5

### Join via lookup (parent-to-child)
SELECT Id, Account.Name FROM Contact WHERE Account.Industry = 'Technology'

### Use IN clause
SELECT Id, Name FROM User WHERE Id IN ('005xx1', '005xx2', '005xx3')

### SOSL text search
FIND {search term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Case(Id, OwnerId, IssueId__c, Subject)

## SOSL Rules
- Valid search scopes: ALL FIELDS, NAME FIELDS, EMAIL FIELDS, PHONE FIELDS
- DO NOT use: IN DESCRIPTION FIELDS (not valid)
- Syntax: FIND {term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Object(Field1, Field2)
- Use SOSL when you need to search text content across records

## Important Constraints (Salesforce-specific)
- The Description field CANNOT be used in WHERE clauses (not filterable)
- Use SOSL with IN ALL FIELDS to search text content instead of WHERE Description LIKE
- Date format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ (e.g., 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)
- String values use single quotes: WHERE Status = 'Closed'
- NULL check: WHERE Field != null
- LIKE operator works on: Subject, Name, Email (NOT on Description, Body, TextBody)
- Relationship fields use dot notation: Account.Name, Owner.Name, Contact.Email
- For User lookups: query User object separately, then use Id in WHERE clause
- CaseHistory__c tracks ownership changes: Field__c = 'Owner Assignment', NewValue__c = User Id
- CANNOT use column aliases in ORDER BY — use the aggregate expression directly
  WRONG: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) cnt FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY cnt DESC
  CORRECT: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY COUNT(Id) DESC
- SOSL search terms cannot contain hyphens — remove or replace them with spaces

## Key Object Relationships
- Case.OwnerId -> User.Id (current case owner)
- Case.IssueId__c -> Issue__c.Id (issue category)
- Case.OrderItemId__c -> OrderItem.Id -> Product2.Id (product linked to case)
- CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c -> Case.Id (case ownership history)
- CaseHistory__c.NewValue__c -> User.Id (agent assigned)
- Order.AccountId -> Account.Id
- OrderItem.Product2Id -> Product2.Id
- Lead.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Opportunity.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Territory2.Description contains comma-separated state codes
- UserTerritory2Association links User to Territory2

## Task-Specific Hints

### Case Routing: Finding agent expertise
- Find issue expertise: query CaseHistory__c or Case WHERE IssueId__c matches and Status = 'Closed', GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find product expertise: join Case -> OrderItem -> Product2, GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find workload: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Status != 'Closed' GROUP BY OwnerId

### Handle Time: Calculating duration
- Handle time = ClosedDate - CreatedDate (query both fields from Case)
- Filter by date range on CreatedDate or ClosedDate

### Transfer Count: Tracking ownership changes
- Use CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c = 'Owner Assignment' to count transfers
- GROUP BY CaseId__c to get transfer count per case

### Lead Routing: Territory-based assignment
- Territory2.Description contains state codes (e.g., 'CA,IL,AL')
- UserTerritory2Association links agents to territories
- Match lead's state to territory, then find associated agent

# Additional task context
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'. 

## Today's date: 2023-12-01

# Domain Details
## Quarters of the Year
- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).
- Q3: July 1 to September 30 (both inclusive).
- Q4: October 1 to December 31 (both inclusive).
## Seasons
- Winter (2024): December 1, 2024 to February 28/29, 2025 (both inclusive), specifying the year to avoid ambiguity.
- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).
- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. (i.e. begins on the date calculated by subtracting six months from the end_date and ends on the end_date) That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.
In the past 6 weeks, which agent had the highest average number of transfers among those handling more than 1 case? Return only the Id of the agent.

<step-back>
- Objects involved: Case, CaseHistory__c, User
- Relationships: Case.OwnerId → User.Id, CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c → Case.Id, CaseHistory__c.NewValue__c → User.Id (for owner assignment)
- Constraints: We need to filter by the past 6 weeks and count transfers per agent.
- Logical sequence: 1) Find all CaseHistory__c records for 'Owner Assignment' in the past 6 weeks, 2) Group by CaseId__c to count transfers per case, 3) Filter cases with more than one transfer, 4) Count transfers per agent, 5) Calculate average transfers per agent handling more than one case, 6) Find the agent with the highest average.
</step-back>
<thought> Starting by finding all CaseHistory__c records for 'Owner Assignment' in the past 6 weeks. </thought>
<execute> SELECT Id, CaseId__c, NewValue__c FROM CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c = 'Owner Assignment' AND CreatedDate >= LAST_N_WEEKS:6 </execute>
Salesforce instance output: []

<thought> Since there are no CaseHistory__c records for 'Owner Assignment' in the past 6 weeks, it means no cases were transferred during this period. Therefore, no agent had any transfers.</thought>
<respond> None </respond>

traj.content:
You are an expert in Salesforce and you have access to a Salesforce instance. You are interacting with the system and an internal user (i.e., an employee of the same company).

# Instructions
- You will be provided a question, the system description, and relevant task context.
- You MUST follow the Step-Back approach:
  1. FIRST, step back and reason abstractly: What Salesforce objects, fields, and relationships are relevant? What constraints exist? What is the logical approach?
  2. THEN, execute SOQL/SOSL queries based on your abstract understanding.
- Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) and Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL) can be used to query the system.
- Your generation should always contain a structured output and NOTHING ELSE. Generate only one action per turn.
- DO NOT generate ANY system observation, you will receive this based on your Action command.
- If multiple records are found matching the requirements mentioned, just return a comma-separated string.
- If no record is found matching the requirements mentioned, just return 'None'.

# Output Format

## On your FIRST turn, step back and reason abstractly:
<step-back>
- What objects are involved? [list relevant Salesforce objects]
- What relationships connect them? [describe the joins/lookups needed]
- What constraints apply? [fields that can't be filtered, syntax rules]
- What is the logical sequence? [what to query first, second, etc.]
</step-back>
<thought> Based on my analysis, I will start by... </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query </execute>

## On SUBSEQUENT turns:
<thought> [reasoning about what I learned and what to do next] </thought>
<execute> a valid SOQL/SOSL query </execute>

## When you have enough information to answer:
<thought> [summarize findings and confirm the answer] </thought>
<respond> final answer </respond>

# Guidelines
- ALWAYS start with <step-back> on your first turn to reason about the problem abstractly.
- In your step-back, identify which objects and fields you need BEFORE writing any query.
- Consider constraints: Description fields cannot be filtered, use SOSL for text search.
- After the first turn, proceed with <thought> and <execute> like normal.
- When you are confident about the answer, submit it with <respond>.
- Always end with a <respond> action containing ONLY the answer, NO full sentences or any explanation.

# Example
Question: Which agent closed the most cases related to "Engine Malfunction" for Volvo XC60?
Output (Turn 1):
<step-back>
- Objects involved: Case, Issue__c, OrderItem, Product2, User
- Relationships: Case.IssueId__c → Issue__c.Id, Case.OrderItemId__c → OrderItem.Id, OrderItem.Product2Id → Product2.Id, Case.OwnerId → User.Id
- Constraints: Description fields are not filterable. Must use Name or SOSL for text search.
- Logical sequence: 1) Find Issue__c matching "Engine Malfunction", 2) Find Product2 matching "Volvo XC60", 3) Query Cases with that IssueId and linked to that product, 4) Group by OwnerId to find top agent
</step-back>
<thought> Starting by finding the Issue__c record for engine malfunction. </thought>
<execute> SELECT Id, Name FROM Issue__c WHERE Name LIKE '%Malfunction%' </execute>

Output (Turn 2):
<thought> Found Issue 'Vehicle Malfunction' with Id 'a03xx1'. Now finding the product. </thought>
<execute> SELECT Id FROM Product2 WHERE Name LIKE '%Volvo XC60%' </execute>

Output (Turn 3):
<thought> Found Product Id. Now finding the agent with most closed cases for this issue. </thought>
<execute> SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE IssueId__c = 'a03xx1' AND Status = 'Closed' GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY COUNT(Id) DESC LIMIT 1 </execute>

# Salesforce instance description
The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2, ProductCategoryProduct, Pricebook2, PricebookEntry, Order, OrderItem, EmailMessage, LiveChatTranscript, Issue__c, CaseHistory__c, Opportunity, OpportunityLineItem, Quote, QuoteLineItem, Contract, VoiceCallTranscript__c, Task, Event, Territory2, UserTerritory2Association, Lead

### SOSL text search
FIND {search term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Case(Id, OwnerId, IssueId__c, Subject)

## SOSL Rules
- Valid search scopes: ALL FIELDS, NAME FIELDS, EMAIL FIELDS, PHONE FIELDS
- DO NOT use: IN DESCRIPTION FIELDS (not valid)
- Syntax: FIND {term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Object(Field1, Field2)
- Use SOSL when you need to search text content across records

## Important Constraints (Salesforce-specific)
- The Description field CANNOT be used in WHERE clauses (not filterable)
- Use SOSL with IN ALL FIELDS to search text content instead of WHERE Description LIKE
- Date format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ (e.g., 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)
- String values use single quotes: WHERE Status = 'Closed'
- NULL check: WHERE Field != null
- LIKE operator works on: Subject, Name, Email (NOT on Description, Body, TextBody)
- Relationship fields use dot notation: Account.Name, Owner.Name, Contact.Email
- For User lookups: query User object separately, then use Id in WHERE clause
- CaseHistory__c tracks ownership changes: Field__c = 'Owner Assignment', NewValue__c = User Id
- CANNOT use column aliases in ORDER BY — use the aggregate expression directly
  WRONG: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) cnt FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY cnt DESC
  CORRECT: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY COUNT(Id) DESC
- SOSL search terms cannot contain hyphens — remove or replace them with spaces

## Key Object Relationships
- Case.OwnerId -> User.Id (current case owner)
- Case.IssueId__c -> Issue__c.Id (issue category)
- Case.OrderItemId__c -> OrderItem.Id -> Product2.Id (product linked to case)
- CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c -> Case.Id (case ownership history)
- CaseHistory__c.NewValue__c -> User.Id (agent assigned)
- Order.AccountId -> Account.Id
- OrderItem.Product2Id -> Product2.Id
- Lead.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Opportunity.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Territory2.Description contains comma-separated state codes
- UserTerritory2Association links User to Territory2

## Task-Specific Hints

### Case Routing: Finding agent expertise
- Find issue expertise: query CaseHistory__c or Case WHERE IssueId__c matches and Status = 'Closed', GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find product expertise: join Case -> OrderItem -> Product2, GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find workload: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Status != 'Closed' GROUP BY OwnerId

### Handle Time: Calculating duration
- Handle time = ClosedDate - CreatedDate (query both fields from Case)
- Filter by date range on CreatedDate or ClosedDate

### Transfer Count: Tracking ownership changes
- Use CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c = 'Owner Assignment' to count transfers
- GROUP BY CaseId__c to get transfer count per case

### Lead Routing: Territory-based assignment
- Territory2.Description contains state codes (e.g., 'CA,IL,AL')
- UserTerritory2Association links agents to territories
- Match lead's state to territory, then find associated agent

# Additional task context
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'. 

## Today's date: 2021-09-23

# Domain Details
## Quarters of the Year
- Q1: January 1 to March 31 (both inclusive).
- Q2: April 1 to June 30 (both inclusive).
- Q3: July 1 to September 30 (both inclusive).
- Q4: October 1 to December 31 (both inclusive).
## Seasons
- Winter (2024): December 1, 2024 to February 28/29, 2025 (both inclusive), specifying the year to avoid ambiguity.
- Spring: March 1 to May 31 (both inclusive).
- Summer: June 1 to August 31 (both inclusive).
- Autumn/Fall: September 1 to November 30 (both inclusive).
## Time Periods
- Past 2 quarters: This refers to any timeframe spanning two quarters back from a specified `end_date`. (i.e. begins on the date calculated by subtracting six months from the end_date and ends on the end_date) That translates to a six-month period retrospectively from the `end_date`.
- Issue Significantly More Than Other Months: This means there is a month where the number of cases reported are larger than all other months.
In the past year, which agent had the lowest average number of transfers among those handling more than 0 cases? Return only the Id of the agent.

<step-back>
- Objects involved: Case, CaseHistory__c, User
- Relationships: Case.OwnerId → User.Id, CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c → Case.Id, CaseHistory__c.NewValue__c → User.Id (for owner changes)
- Constraints: We need to count the number of transfers per agent and filter out agents with 0 cases.
- Logical sequence: 1) Find all case ownership changes in the past year, 2) Count the number of transfers per agent, 3) Filter out agents with 0 cases, 4) Calculate average transfers per agent, 5) Find the agent with the lowest average
</step-back>

WHERE ch.Field__c = 'Owner Assignment' AND ch.CreatedDate >= 2020-09-23T00:00:00Z AND ch.CreatedDate <= 2021-09-23T23:59:59Z 
GROUP BY ch.NewValue__c 
HAVING COUNT(c.Id) > 0 </execute>

# Instructions
- You will be provided a question, the system description, and relevant task context.
- You MUST follow the Step-Back approach:
  1. FIRST, step back and reason abstractly: What Salesforce objects, fields, and relationships are relevant? What constraints exist? What is the logical approach?
  2. THEN, execute SOQL/SOSL queries based on your abstract understanding.
- Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) and Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL) can be used to query the system.
- Your generation should always contain a structured output and NOTHING ELSE. Generate only one action per turn.
- DO NOT generate ANY system observation, you will receive this based on your Action command.
- If multiple records are found matching the requirements mentioned, just return a comma-separated string.
- If no record is found matching the requirements mentioned, just return 'None'.

- Relationships: Case.IssueId__c → Issue__c.Id, Case.OrderItemId__c → OrderItem.Id, OrderItem.Product2Id → Product2.Id, Case.OwnerId → User.Id
- Constraints: Description fields are not filterable. Must use Name or SOSL for text search.

# Salesforce instance description
The objects available in the Salesforce instance are:
User, Account, Contact, Case, Knowledge__kav, ProductCategory, Product2, ProductCategoryProduct, Pricebook2, PricebookEntry, Order, OrderItem, EmailMessage, LiveChatTranscript, Issue__c, CaseHistory__c, Opportunity, OpportunityLineItem, Quote, QuoteLineItem, Contract, VoiceCallTranscript__c, Task, Event, Territory2, UserTerritory2Association, Lead

## The fields available for the objects along with their descriptions and dependencies are:
User
  - Alias: Alias, usually a shortened version of the agent's name
  - Email: Email address of the agent
  - EmailEncodingKey: Email encoding, usually 'UTF-8'
  - FirstName: First name of the agent
  - Id: ID of the user
  - LanguageLocaleKey: Language locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - LastName: Last name of the agent
  - LocaleSidKey: Locale, e.g., 'en_US'
  - Phone: Phone number of the agent
  - ProfileId: ID of the user profile, typically 'Standard User' or a similar profile
  - TimeZoneSidKey: Time zone, e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'
  - Username: Unique username for the agent in Salesforce
Account
  - Description: Detailed description of the account
  - FirstName: First name of the account
  - Id: ID of the account
  - LastName: Last name of the account
  - Name: Name of the account
  - PersonEmail: Email address of the person associated with the account
  - Phone: Phone number of the account
  - ShippingState: State for shipping address
Contact
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - Id: ID of the contact
Case
  - AccountId: References Account object
  - ClosedDate: Date when the case was closed in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - ContactId: ID of the contact associated with this case. References Contact object
  - CreatedDate: Date when the case was created in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' format
  - Description: Detailed description of the case
  - Id: ID of the case
  - IssueId__c: ID of the Issue associated with this case
  - OrderItemId__c: ID of the Order Item associated with this case
  - OwnerId: The latest owner of the case. References User object
  - Priority: Priority of the case
  - Status: Status of the case, e.g., 'New', 'Working', 'Escalated', etc
  - Subject: Subject of the case
Knowledge__kav
  - FAQ_Answer__c: Content of the knowledge article
  - Id: ID of the knowledge article
  - Summary: Summary of the article
  - Title: Title of the knowledge article
  - UrlName: Unique URL for the article
ProductCategory
  - CatalogId: ID of the catalog this category belongs to
  - Id: ID of the product category
  - Name: Name of the product category
Product2
  - Description: Description of the product
  - External_ID__c: External ID for mapping purposes
  - Id: ID of the product
  - IsActive: Boolean to mark if the product is active
  - Name: Name of the product
ProductCategoryProduct
  - Id: ID of the product category product
  - ProductCategoryId: References ProductCategory object

Pricebook2

PricebookEntry
  - Id: ID of the price book entry

  - Product2Id: ID of the product associated with this price book entry. References Product2 object
  - UnitPrice: Unit price of the product
Order
  - AccountId: ID of the customer account. References Account object
  - EffectiveDate: Effective date of the order in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format

OrderItem
  - Id: ID of the order item

EmailMessage

LiveChatTranscript

Issue__c
  - Description__c: Detailed description of the issue

CaseHistory__c

OpportunityLineItem

QuoteLineItem

Contract

VoiceCallTranscript__c
  - Body__c: Content of the voice call transcript

Task

# SOQL/SOSL Quick Reference

## SOQL Syntax
SELECT field1, field2 FROM Object WHERE condition ORDER BY field LIMIT n

## Common Patterns

### Count records
SELECT COUNT() FROM Case WHERE Status = 'Open'

### Filter by date range
SELECT Id FROM Case WHERE CreatedDate >= 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z AND CreatedDate <= 2025-03-31T23:59:59Z

### Group and aggregate
SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) total FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId HAVING COUNT(Id) > 5

### Join via lookup (parent-to-child)
SELECT Id, Account.Name FROM Contact WHERE Account.Industry = 'Technology'

### Use IN clause
SELECT Id, Name FROM User WHERE Id IN ('005xx1', '005xx2', '005xx3')

### SOSL text search
FIND {search term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Case(Id, OwnerId, IssueId__c, Subject)

## SOSL Rules
- Valid search scopes: ALL FIELDS, NAME FIELDS, EMAIL FIELDS, PHONE FIELDS
- DO NOT use: IN DESCRIPTION FIELDS (not valid)
- Syntax: FIND {term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Object(Field1, Field2)
- Use SOSL when you need to search text content across records

## Important Constraints (Salesforce-specific)
- The Description field CANNOT be used in WHERE clauses (not filterable)
- Use SOSL with IN ALL FIELDS to search text content instead of WHERE Description LIKE
- Date format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ (e.g., 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z)
- String values use single quotes: WHERE Status = 'Closed'
- NULL check: WHERE Field != null
- LIKE operator works on: Subject, Name, Email (NOT on Description, Body, TextBody)
- Relationship fields use dot notation: Account.Name, Owner.Name, Contact.Email
- For User lookups: query User object separately, then use Id in WHERE clause
- CaseHistory__c tracks ownership changes: Field__c = 'Owner Assignment', NewValue__c = User Id
- CANNOT use column aliases in ORDER BY — use the aggregate expression directly
  WRONG: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) cnt FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY cnt DESC
  CORRECT: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY COUNT(Id) DESC
- SOSL search terms cannot contain hyphens — remove or replace them with spaces

## Key Object Relationships
- Case.OwnerId -> User.Id (current case owner)
- Case.IssueId__c -> Issue__c.Id (issue category)
- Case.OrderItemId__c -> OrderItem.Id -> Product2.Id (product linked to case)
- CaseHistory__c.CaseId__c -> Case.Id (case ownership history)
- CaseHistory__c.NewValue__c -> User.Id (agent assigned)
- Order.AccountId -> Account.Id
- OrderItem.Product2Id -> Product2.Id
- Lead.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Opportunity.OwnerId -> User.Id
- Territory2.Description contains comma-separated state codes
- UserTerritory2Association links User to Territory2

## Task-Specific Hints

### Case Routing: Finding agent expertise
- Find issue expertise: query CaseHistory__c or Case WHERE IssueId__c matches and Status = 'Closed', GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find product expertise: join Case -> OrderItem -> Product2, GROUP BY OwnerId
- Find workload: SELECT OwnerId, COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Status != 'Closed' GROUP BY OwnerId

### Handle Time: Calculating duration
- Handle time = ClosedDate - CreatedDate (query both fields from Case)
- Filter by date range on CreatedDate or ClosedDate

### Transfer Count: Tracking ownership changes
- Use CaseHistory__c WHERE Field__c = 'Owner Assignment' to count transfers
- GROUP BY CaseId__c to get transfer count per case

### Lead Routing: Territory-based assignment
- Territory2.Description contains state codes (e.g., 'CA,IL,AL')
- UserTerritory2Association links agents to territories
- Match lead's state to territory, then find associated agent

# Additional task context
## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.
